behavior_summary: When the tapestry item is examined (looked at), the script prepares object/process context, constructs the literal string "tapestry ", and invokes the item's bark routine to produce the short description visible to the player.
Context setup includes setting item info/process exclusion so the bark is emitted with appropriate context (who sees it or how it is attributed).
The temporary string is freed after the bark call, and the bark return value is preserved and propagated as the handler's return value (after an implied control check).
notable_control_flow: Entry on a U8P_TAPESTNS::look/examine event: setup/init opcodes run to prepare context (set info and process-exclude operations).
Push current process/player id (pid) and the literal string "tapestry " as arguments, then call Item::bark on the target item.
Free the temporary string, handle the bark return value (store/inspect), perform an 'implies' style operation, and then suspend/return while propagating the bark result.
open_questions: The exact engine semantics of the 'set info' and 'process exclude' opcodes used before barking — what precise audience or visibility rules they enforce is inferred but not canonical.
The meaning of the 'implies' operation applied to the bark return value — whether it gates additional behavior or is purely stack housekeeping is uncertain.
Whether BP+06h absolutely refers to the tapestry item instance (rather than the actor/process invoking the U8P_TAPESTNS::look) is strongly likely but technically inferred from calling convention.

main_function.name: U8P_TAPESTNS::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the 'U8P_TAPESTNS::look' (examine) interaction for the tapestry item by emitting a short descriptive bark ('tapestry ') through Item::bark after preparing per-process/item context, then return the bark result.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — invoked when a player/actor performs a 'U8P_TAPESTNS::look' (examine) on the tapestry item.

function_map.name: U8P_TAPESTNS::look
function_map.role: Process event handler for the tapestry's 'U8P_TAPESTNS::look' interaction; prepares context and delegates to Item::bark to present the description.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine-provided routine used to emit/display a short text message from an item/process to a player/process (used here to show the tapestry description).

external_calls.purpose: Emit/display the short description string 'tapestry ' to the relevant process/player; receives the target item/process reference (BP+06h) and a temporary string argument.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the tapestry item is examined (looked at), the script prepares object/process context, constructs the literal string "tapestry ", and invokes the item's bark routine to produce the short description visible to the player.
